message:
Welcome to Version Confusion, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

? *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 ? *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once youre ready just click start to begin
Hey guys im seeing an issue with the website loading slowly
Anyone else experaning this?
No all ok for me
No issue for me
What browser and version of it are you using Tanya?
Im using chrome and let me check the version
Ok if it is V105.0 this is a recorded bug with a ticket in the backlog
Yeah it is V105.0
Should we not raise an incident for this? I know I can update the version and it will stop effecting me.
But could this not be effecting customers?
Had a look at the market share of people using this version it is only at 0.09%
Still not sure if it should not be an incidnt. @emma.allsopp-options-it.com What do you think?
I can access ok too, @bob any complaints on the customer front?
:star: Attempt to replicate the issue
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
We’ve had a couple of customers mention slow loading on Chrome
I don’t have numbers yet, but I can check reports
0.09% is a low amount of people using this version, @tanya is there anyway we can deploy the update for everyone? @bob yes pleasee check reports so we can see how many people this is affecting
Might just be legacy browser quirks
@maya are you seeing any issues with anything loading?
It would require planning in order to avoid service disruption and other possible conflicts
@bob please report back asap once you know the impact of this
:star: Manage actions and timings
I’ve pulled some numbers — this is bigger than expected
About 20% of sessions are showing elevated load times
Seeing some timeouts as well
That’s much larger than Chrome 105’s share
That suggests this might affect more versions than we thought
@tanya are we sure this is related to the older verison V105.0
:star: Reviewed changes
Checking things on my end
I’m seeing TTFB differences between browser versions
20%? That’s surprising
That wouldn’t align with just Chrome 105
*Incident Number INC100090:* p2 20% users having elevated load times due to version of website
:star: Incident record created
Maybe detection is mislabeling browser versions?
Really dont think we need an incident. As I said we have a bug already and really need to focus on delivery of new features.
I’m not seeing spikes in payment retries
@daniel can you please check to see what other factors may be causing this
@shay can i get a list of recent changes deployed
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Are customers abandoning sessions?
@bob are customers not buying?
Nothing on my end would indicate this to be a scopes issue or any services being blocked
@bez user activity is still high
We see decline in revenue compared to last 2 weeks
@bob is this affecting 20% of customers in a certain region?
It seems that trends was build up for that period of time
Seems to be global
@daniel can i please get report back?
@shay can I please get those changes?
Can someone confirm when this trend began?
@bob when did reports start comign in?

sure
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/ZDQ1MTE3NWU5ZDYxNDA4MTkxZmM3ZGJmMGJhNmE1OTE
Currently investigating this cause
Hi everyone can we please get everyone's attention here - we are having a global issue
First report was mentioned as far as 2 weeks ago
Global issue - first reported 2 weeks ago
Could be a frontend performance regression
investigating recent changes on platform will report back in 5 mins
If checkout forms are timing out, maybe retry logic is triggering delays.
:star: Track actions and time box them
The delay appears before JS execution
That makes frontend less likely as root cause.
@hamed do you have an idea of the root casue?
Just joined the chat
what do we know so far?
20% users having elevated load times due to version of website - the delay appears before JS execution, That makes frontend less likely as root cause.
:star: Qualify the problem
:star: Form a working theory
first report is 2 weeks ago also
@bob any further reports or still affecting only 20%
I have reviewed latest changes within 24h, nothing stands out
If this is browser-specific and global, we should check proxy behavior.
@maya can you check proxy behaviour please
@tanya can you also help maya with this please
trend started two weeks ago — timing doesn’t match, agree most likely not anything recently deployed
On it
:thumbsup:
changes in last 24hrs do not seem to have impacted this as first report is 2 weeks ago
:star: Facts over opinion
Reviewing NGINX version history now.
There was a version upgrade around two weeks ago
That aligns more closely with impact timeline
Correlation with timeline is key.
Focus on changes closest to when trend began

Currently reviewing NGINX version history now.
There was a version upgrade around two weeks ago
That aligns more closely with impact timeline
@hamed does this appear to be the issue?
Have we notified the customers about this?
Confirmed — NGINX version update introduced compatibility issue.
Older Chrome versions are experiencing delayed request handling.
@tanya and @maya any update on proxy?
Some headers/feature negotiation aren’t fully supported
Still nothing

Confirmed — NGINX version update introduced compatibility issue.

Older Chrome versions are experiencing delayed request handling.
That explains the TTFB differences I was seeing.
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
Notifying customers.
Frontend itself is behaving normally.
So frontend was fine — issue was before it even loaded
That explains why bundle analysis didn’t show regression.

@bob  can you please notify customers that Confirmed — NGINX version update introduced compatibility issue.

Older Chrome versions are experiencing delayed request handling

@bob an you please notify customers that Confirmed — NGINX version update introduced compatibility issue.

Older Chrome versions are experiencing delayed request handling
@hamed how can we resolve this
Certainly!
:star: Escalating when required
We can either roll back NGINX or patch config
Bob has notified customers, working hamed to resolve issue
Rollback is fastest, lowest risk. but that's only a temp fix
lets roll back for now as customer experience is priority and getting the site working as normal
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
then we can resolve root cause after
Agree, I will work on the rollback and patch the service later
@hamed / @tanya can we please roll back?
I’ll monitor latency by browser version post-deploy.
We are working to roll back NGINX to resolve issue for the moment
@tanya let me know once completed thank you
Just double checking everything before the rollback
Tanya working on roll back will update once completed.
Rollback in progress.....
Let’s confirm stability before declaring full recovery
This is not a permanent fix however will restore the site for customers and we can resolve root cause once restored
Rollback complete
@hamed everything look good on your end?
timeouts and delay about is dropping
Looks like we are recovering
this has not resolved the issue?
Hey @emma.allsopp-options-it.com I have just retried the website with V105.0 of chrome and it seems the fix worked
Glad we created an incident for this. Need to review our training on when to create an incident or a bug but great work team.
Sorry guys should have raised an incident when first discovered.
oh great!
We are all learning Daniel. We can carry the learnings foward
Just checked and it looks all good now team
Lets continue monitoring this and we can have a great conversation during the PIR
@bob can we confirm site is working as normal?
Sales metrics are improving

Thank you for playing Version Confusion, We recommend that you take 5 minutes to go and get a drink or stretch your legs.
Once you are back we ask that you complete this retrospective and feedback form, just hit start when you are ready.
Roll back complete, Tanya has retried the website with V105.0 of chrome and it seems the fix worked

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
